---
title: "Dataset Presentation"
---

This post introduces the dataset we used in our experiments to predict the one-year mortality risk of hospital patients.

### Data Origin and Credits

This dataset is a publicly available synthetic dataset based on real patient records from the CIUSSS de l'Estrie-CHUS in Sherbrooke, Canada.

To protect patient privacy, the original data was mapped to synthetic patients using the AVATAR method. This approach creates a group of synthetic patients that share the same statistical properties as the original patients.

You can find more details and download the dataset directly from its source: <https://zenodo.org/records/12954673>

### Dataset Overview

- It represents 123,646 synthetic patients.
- There are 248,485 rows, each corresponding to a single hospital visit.
- The raw data contains 248 columns. After excluding the first two and last two columns, we have **244 predictors** to train our models.
